title: thymus munbyanus subsp. ciliatus
intro: Thymus munbyanus subsp. ciliatus, also known as Munby's thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Munby's thyme include relief from respiratory issues such as colds and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Munby's thyme can be found in teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide various health benefits.
